title: The Care Conversation, Calm
subtitle: A visualisation of having the parent-money conversation without the emotional charge
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation of a clear, calm conversation with ageing parents about money and care. You picture the exchange as it could go when the emotional charge has been released: direct, clear, kind.

practice.whenToUse: Use before a real conversation about money and care with ageing parents, or to reduce the anxiety charge that prevents the conversation from happening.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation for the conversation that is needed but hasn't happened yet. You picture how it goes when the charge has been released: direct, clear, kind. Not the anxious version — the clear one.

Picture a room where the conversation could happen. Their kitchen, your kitchen, a phone call. You and a parent, or both parents, sitting or standing in a way that feels ordinary. Nothing is dramatized. The conversation is not an intervention. It is two or three sentences said clearly.

You say the first sentence. The one you've been not saying. You say it plainly — without apology in the voice, without the pre-emptive softening that tends to obscure the actual point. Your parent hears it. There is a pause. The conversation continues.
Notice that the room is still intact. Nothing has broken. The relationship has not ended. The conversation that was too risky to have is now in the room, and the room is handling it.

Open your eyes when ready. If the fear reduced even slightly during the visualisation, that is useful information. Write one line about what the clear conversation would actually need to contain. That line is the preparation.
